source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Thermal burn injury from heated Glycol splash
actions_taken: IP evacuated via helicopter to onshore heliport
IP transported to Plaquemines Medical Center in Port Sulphur, LA
Medical treatment provided including cleaning and Neosporin application
IP placed on restricted work duty for three days
WOG issued Operational Safety Alert OSA-2023-014 on 19 July 2023
event.summary: On 3 July 2023 at 1545 hours, an Island Operating Company employee was testing the Level Safety High/Low (LSHL) on the Glycol Reboiler (EBC 2151) at MP 270 Platform B when a blockage in the bridle occurred. As the IP cleared the obstruction, heated Triethylene Glycol at 350°F drained out of the bridle and splashed onto the IP, resulting in severe 2nd degree burns to approximately 27% of the body (abdomen, arms, and side). The IP was evacuated and treated at an onshore medical facility, then placed on restricted work duty for three days.
recommendations: Ensure proper PPE is worn when working with hot liquids and chemicals in accordance with MSDS requirements
Improve Job Safety Analysis to identify thermal hazards associated with hot liquids and chemicals
Include 'Other Hazards' section in JSA to address potential blocked or plugged equipment scenarios
Provide thermal-protective PPE suitable for 350°F liquids rather than ambient temperature protection only

threats.name: Heated Glycol splash during blockage clearing

consequences.name: Severe thermal burn injury to personnel
Personnel incapacity and restricted work duty

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures prior to work
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: the JSA acknowledged the presence of a hazardous substance but missed the section of the JSA form 'Other Hazards' and the possible obstacle of having to 'manage potential blocked or plugged equipment'
the possibility of being splashed by heated Glycol was overlooked

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from thermal and chemical hazards
controls.lod_basis: PPE as secondary barrier to prevent direct contact with hazardous substance

supporting_text: The IP was wearing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at the time of the incident, and though it was adequate for working with ambient temperature liquids, it was not suitable for working with the 350°F Glycol
the PPE worn by the IP was inadequate and should have provided protection for chemical risk and hot liquid (thermal) risk
IP should have had on PPE that was in accordance with the MSDS for Triethylene Glycol as well as protection from the thermal risk of the hot Glycol

controls.name: Testing procedures for LSHL
controls.barrier_role: Provide safe work instructions for equipment testing
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to guide safe execution of maintenance tasks

supporting_text: the IP did follow the proper testing procedures provided by IOC

controls.name: Supervision and hazard awareness
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel awareness of hazards and proper PPE use
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory oversight to ensure hazard recognition and control implementation

supporting_text: Supervision – Not ensuring the use of proper PPE: IP should have had on PPE that was in accordance with the MSDS for Triethylene Glycol as well as protection from the thermal risk of the hot Glycol

controls.name: Operational Safety Alert (OSA-2023-014)
controls.barrier_role: Communicate lessons learned and raise awareness of thermal hazards
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident communication to prevent recurrence across organization

supporting_text: WOG sent out an Operational Safety Alert (OSA-2023-014) on 19 July 2023, to all employees and contractors working on WOG facilities. The title of the OSA was 'Preventing Hot Liquid and Chemical Injuries'
the safety alert also highlighted the proper PPE that should be worn when working with hot liquids and chemicals to raise awareness to all personnel

controls.name: Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) for Triethylene Glycol
controls.barrier_role: Provide hazard information and safe handling requirements
controls.lod_basis: Information control to communicate chemical hazards and required protections

supporting_text: IP should have had on PPE that was in accordance with the MSDS for Triethylene Glycol as well as protection from the thermal risk of the hot Glycol

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
